etymology.description: 兩東 'two bundles' (similar objects) + 囗 added, which corrupted into 甘 (seal script became 曰) -> 𣍘 (intermediate form) -> 曹
etymology.interpretation: The character 𣍘 started as an ideogrammic compound representing the concept of 'pair' or 'same kind.' It was visually conveyed through two bundles resembling 東. The addition of a distinguishing outline 囗, evolving through 甘 and 曰 in different scripts, resulted in the intermediate form 𣍘, leading to the final form 曹. This ideogrammic structure highlights the association with shared or equal entities.
